title: Magazine Final Evangelion Film Slated for Fall Winter 2015
display_title: Magazine Final Evangelion Film Slated for Fall Winter 2015
wikitext:
[]: Magazine: Final Evangelion Film Slated for Fall-Winter 2015             Quote Post by Dima » Fri Dec 26, 2014 2:07 am Part of the article ''The first 2015 issue of Bungeishunjū's Weekly Bunshun magazine on Thursday featured an article on the return of singer Hikaru Utada. It quoted a record company source as saying that confidential talks are underway regarding her return to the music industry. The source added that it has been decided that she will sing the theme song for the final new Evangelion film "slated to open between fall and winter of next year." The source explained that Utada has one more album left on her contract with Universal Music Japan, so the company wants the Evangelion song to be the focus of the final album.'' More info here: [url]https://www.animenewsnetwork.com/news/2014-12-26/magazine-final-evangelion-film-slated-for-fall-winter-2015-with-utada-song/.82629[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Fri Dec 26, 2014 2:35 am It's very important to take note that there has not been any official announcement from Khara as to when Shin is going to be released. Evangelion.co.jp? Nothing. Eva-info.jp? Nothing. Official Twitter pages? Nothing. I'll believe it when I see Khara say it.

[]:             Quote Post by vito » Fri Dec 26, 2014 4:32 am Why are you forcing me to give ANN a click? Copy the whole bloody text which they copied/translated from here anyways: http://neweva.blog103.fc2.com/blog-entry-3498.html which is where us others at evageeks usually get news from.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Dec 26, 2014 4:35 am Ooh! We're at that “I'll believe it when I see it from Khara" stage of sharing news. Something exciting must be brewing!

[]:             Quote Post by Oz » Fri Dec 26, 2014 5:22 am vito wrote: Why are you forcing me to give ANN a click? Copy the whole bloody text which they copied/translated from here anyways: http://neweva.blog103.fc2.com/blog-entry-3498.html which is where us others at evageeks usually get news from.

[]:             Quote Post by airman4 » Fri Dec 26, 2014 6:14 am i'm not ready we all gonna to die before to see it let's be serious here why the rage about ANN ? it's because they gave wrong infos on aoki uru ? really guys ?

[]:             Quote Post by Dima » Fri Dec 26, 2014 6:23 am It's not official, the info it's based on magazine's source. Winter 2015 covers the period from December 2015 till February 2016. Maybe we will get the release date from the official site with the New Year, just like Q.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Fri Dec 26, 2014 8:54 am This is the only quote that should have been posted in the thread from the always trustworthy anonymous quote person. However, the source said the theme song tie-in is entirely dependent on director Hideaki Anno opening the film next year as planned.

[]:             Quote Post by KnightmareX13 » Fri Dec 26, 2014 11:15 am Wonder if Hikaru Utada will do a acoustic version of Sakura Nagashi?

[]:             Quote Post by Ah Q » Fri Dec 26, 2014 12:28 pm vito wrote: Why are you forcing me to give ANN a click? Copy the whole bloody text which they copied/translated from here anyways: http://neweva.blog103.fc2.com/blog-entry-3498.html which is where us others at evageeks usually get news from.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Fri Dec 26, 2014 1:04 pm Google Chrome speaks it adequately for many.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Dec 26, 2014 1:34 pm Let's all just take a , okay? There's nothing for anybody to get worked up about as long as sources are cited/no credit-stealing. Anyway, there's plenty of browser extensions out there to help with Japanese, for sure. And oddly enough, Bing Translator's more spot-on than Google Translate these days (though you still shouldn't use 'em for accuracy) with Japanese...I don't understand the why, myself. TM has the right of it in terms of when release dates are happening (we don't know until Studio khara tells us) so all we can do is guess wildly for now. Or just wait and see.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Fri Dec 26, 2014 4:13 pm vito wrote: Why are you forcing me to give ANN a click? Copy the whole bloody text which they copied/translated from here anyways.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Fri Dec 26, 2014 4:15 pm ANN doesn't have a good reputation, I believe-- but I honestly wonder why myself, since I've never actually gotten a concrete answer (or if I have, I've long since forgotten.)

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Dec 26, 2014 4:24 pm "Historical reasons", I believe (old prejudices based on a few long-ago incidents, probably).

[]:             Quote Post by Stan » Sat Dec 27, 2014 1:57 am I would actually be very surprised if the film makes the big screen in fall 2015. I think it wont, but there is always hope. Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: ANN doesn't have a good reputation, I believe-- but I honestly wonder why myself, since I've never actually gotten a concrete answer (or if I have, I've long since forgotten.)

[]:             Quote Post by Jäeger » Sat Dec 27, 2014 5:08 am Stan wrote: I would actually be very surprised if the film makes the big screen in fall 2015. I think it wont, but there is always hope.

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Sat Dec 27, 2014 6:56 am ^ Princess Kaguya and The Wind Rises have been in production for almost 5 years. So... is there any real alternative to ANN anyways? (f.i. a news site who doesn't cite ANN as source all the time)

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Dec 27, 2014 7:02 am Going direct to the Japanese sources.

[]:             Quote Post by Atropos » Sat Dec 27, 2014 9:13 am When did this become a discussion about ethics in anime journalism?

The source explained that Utada has one more album left on her contract with Universal Music Japan, so the company wants the Evangelion song to be the focus of the final album.''
